nodes.name: HMC
nodes.description: Ingested pipeline from HMC

nodes.name: InitializeHMCState
nodes.description: Construct immutable HMC state and static kernel parameters, including explicit RNG state (PRNGKey/seed-derived state).

inputs.name: seed
inputs.constraints: optional; when provided initializes RNG state

outputs.name: hmc_state_0
outputs.constraints: contains positions, logp_current, gradient, rng_state, trace

outputs.name: kernel_static
outputs.constraints: contains target, step_size, n_leapfrog, mass_matrix (implicit or explicit)

nodes.type_signature: (target: callable log_prob oracle, initial_positions: array[float], step_size: float, n_leapfrog: int, seed: int) -> tuple[StateModelSpec, object]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Reproducible Gradient-Kernel State Bootstrap. Builds an immutable initial state for a gradient-informed iterative transition process by evaluating a differentiable scalar-field oracle at an initial tensor point and storing the point, scalar value, local derivative, random generator state, and trace container. In parallel, it factorizes constant transition hyperparameters into a static kernel object so per-iteration updates can reuse fixed settings without reconfiguration. If a seed is provided, stochastic behavior is made exactly replayable under identical inputs and numeric conditions.. Applications: Computational chemistry: initializing reproducible trajectory-based exploration of high-dimensional energy landscapes for molecular conformations., Geophysical inverse modeling: bootstrapping iterative state transitions for parameter-field reconstruction from indirect sensor measurements., Robotics system identification: initializing gradient-informed stochastic search over dynamic model parameters with replayable randomness., Medical image reconstruction: setting up reproducible iterative posterior exploration for latent volume parameters under differentiable forward models.

nodes.name: LeapfrogProposalKernel
nodes.description: Pure Hamiltonian proposal transition: consumes current position/momenta and returns proposed position/momenta plus refreshed log-probability gradient.

outputs.name: proposal_state_out
outputs.constraints: new pos, new momenta, updated logp, updated gradient

nodes.type_signature: (proposal_state_in: StateModelSpec, kernel_static: object, log_prob_oracle: callable) -> StateModelSpec
nodes.conceptual_summary: Symplectic Latent-State Integrator. Applies a fixed number of leapfrog integration steps to a coupled state made of a primary vector and an auxiliary velocity-like vector, using gradients from a scalar potential oracle. Each step alternates half/full updates so the map is approximately energy-conserving while remaining explicit and numerically stable for moderate step sizes. The result is a deterministically transformed state with the scalar potential value and gradient recomputed at the terminal primary vector.. Applications: Molecular simulation: integrating particle configuration and velocity states under differentiable force fields., Robotics trajectory optimization: proposing dynamically consistent perturbations in high-dimensional configuration spaces., Bayesian neural parameter exploration: generating long-range proposals in weight space for posterior sampling., Inverse imaging/geophysics: traversing latent model spaces with gradient-informed, low-random-walk state transitions.

nodes.name: PrepareDifferentiableStateAndFirstHalfKick
nodes.description: For one leapfrog iteration, re-enable AD tracking on position and apply the first half-step momentum update using the carried gradient summands from the previous state.

inputs.name: last_grad_summands
inputs.constraints: Scaled gradient half-step from previous state

nodes.type_signature: (position_in: Tensor<B, 2>, momentum_in: Tensor<B, 2>, last_grad_summands: Tensor<B, 2>) -> tuple[Tensor<B, 2>, Tensor<B, 2>]

nodes.name: DriftPositionByFullStep
nodes.description: Advance position by a full drift step using the half-kicked momentum and re-enable AD tracking on the new position.
nodes.type_signature: (position_ad: Tensor<B, 2>, momentum_half_kicked: Tensor<B, 2>, step_size: T) -> Tensor<B, 2>

nodes.name: RecomputeGradientHalfKickContribution
nodes.description: Evaluate target log-probability at the advanced position, backpropagate to obtain gradient, and scale it into the next half-step momentum contribution.
nodes.type_signature: (position_advanced: Tensor<B, 2>, log_prob_oracle: callable, step_size: T) -> tuple[Tensor<B, 1>, Tensor<B, 2>]

nodes.name: CompleteMomentumKickAndRefreshCarry
nodes.description: Apply the second half-step momentum kick using the newly computed gradient summands and emit the next iteration carry state.

outputs.name: position_out
outputs.constraints: Position carry for next leapfrog iteration

outputs.name: momentum_out
outputs.constraints: Momentum carry for next leapfrog iteration

outputs.name: last_grad_summands_out
outputs.constraints: Gradient carry for next leapfrog iteration

nodes.type_signature: (position_advanced: Tensor<B, 2>, momentum_half_kicked: Tensor<B, 2>, grad_summands: Tensor<B, 2>) -> tuple[Tensor<B, 2>, Tensor<B, 2>, Tensor<B, 2>]

nodes.name: ComputeTerminalLogProbAndEmitProposal
nodes.description: After repeating the leapfrog transition exactly n_leapfrog times, compute final log-probability at the terminal position and return detached proposal tensors.

outputs.name: proposal_state_out
outputs.constraints: Contains detached position, detached momentum, detached final log-probability

nodes.type_signature: (position_final: Tensor<B, 2>, momentum_final: Tensor<B, 2>, log_prob_oracle: callable) -> StateModelSpec

nodes.name: MetropolisHMCTransition
nodes.description: Single pure HMC kernel step: samples fresh momenta from RNG state, invokes leapfrog proposal, performs accept/reject, and returns new immutable chain state.

inputs.name: chain_state_in
inputs.constraints: contains positions, logp_current, gradient, rng_state, trace

outputs.name: transition_stats
outputs.constraints: accept flag, acceptance prob, Hamiltonian delta

nodes.type_signature: (chain_state_in: StateModelSpec, kernel_static: object, proposal_state_out: StateModelSpec) -> tuple[StateModelSpec, object]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Gradient-Guided Symplectic Metropolis Transition. Consumes an immutable Markov state containing a continuous parameter vector, local energy value, energy gradient, and pseudorandom generator state, then draws an auxiliary momentum vector from a reference distribution. It combines this momentum with a precomputed symplectic proposal to form a candidate state and applies a Metropolis accept/reject test using the Hamiltonian difference to preserve the target invariant measure. The output is a new immutable state (candidate or retained previous state) plus diagnostic transition statistics.. Applications: Sampling high-dimensional molecular conformation states in computational chemistry from an energy-defined distribution., Posterior exploration of latent field parameters in geophysical or climate inverse models with differentiable forward operators., Bayesian system identification for nonlinear dynamical models in robotics or control, where gradients of trajectory likelihood are available., Probabilistic reconstruction in medical imaging (e.g., differentiable tomography) by drawing from constrained high-dimensional posterior densities.

nodes.name: infer_chain_geometry
nodes.description: Extract chain count and latent dimension from the current position tensor shape.

inputs.name: chain_state_in
inputs.constraints: contains positions tensor with shape [n_chains, dim]

nodes.type_signature: (chain_state_in: StateModelSpec) -> tuple[usize, usize, Tensor<B,2>]

nodes.name: sample_initial_momenta
nodes.description: Sample fresh Gaussian momenta for each chain and dimension.

inputs.name: kernel_static
inputs.constraints: provides tensor backend/device and RNG source

nodes.type_signature: (n_chains: usize, dim: usize, kernel_static: object) -> Tensor<B,2>

nodes.name: evaluate_current_logp_and_halfstep_gradient
nodes.description: Evaluate current unnormalized log-density and compute the half-step gradient summands used by leapfrog.
nodes.type_signature: (positions_current: Tensor<B,2>, kernel_static: object) -> tuple[Tensor<B,1>, Tensor<B,2>]

nodes.name: compute_current_hamiltonian
nodes.description: Compute kinetic energy from sampled momenta and form current Hamiltonian H = -logp + KE.
nodes.type_signature: (momentum_0: Tensor<B,2>, logp_current: Tensor<B,1>) -> tuple[Tensor<B,1>, Tensor<B,1>]

nodes.name: propose_state_with_leapfrog
nodes.description: Run leapfrog integration from current positions and sampled momenta to obtain proposed state and proposed log-density.

inputs.name: grad_summands
inputs.constraints: half-step gradient term needed by integrator state

inputs.name: kernel_static
inputs.constraints: contains leapfrog hyperparameters and target model

nodes.type_signature: (positions_current: Tensor<B,2>, momentum_0: Tensor<B,2>, grad_summands: Tensor<B,2>, kernel_static: object) -> tuple[Tensor<B,2>, Tensor<B,2>, Tensor<B,1>]

nodes.name: compute_proposed_hamiltonian
nodes.description: Compute proposed kinetic energy and proposed Hamiltonian for Metropolis acceptance.
nodes.type_signature: (proposed_momenta: Tensor<B,2>, logp_proposed: Tensor<B,1>) -> tuple[Tensor<B,1>, Tensor<B,1>]

nodes.name: sample_log_uniform_thresholds
nodes.description: Draw one uniform variate per chain and convert to log-space acceptance thresholds.
nodes.type_signature: (n_chains: usize, kernel_static: object) -> Tensor<B,1>

nodes.name: apply_metropolis_accept_reject
nodes.description: Compute acceptance log-ratio, build mask, and select accepted proposed positions against current positions.
nodes.type_signature: (h_current: Tensor<B,1>, h_proposed: Tensor<B,1>, ln_u: Tensor<B,1>, positions_current: Tensor<B,2>, proposed_positions: Tensor<B,2>) -> tuple[Tensor<B,2>, Tensor<B,1,Bool>, Tensor<B,1>]

nodes.name: assemble_transition_result
nodes.description: Return the immutable next chain state and transition diagnostics.

outputs.name: chain_state_out
outputs.constraints: same structure as input with updated positions and cached grad state

nodes.type_signature: (chain_state_in: StateModelSpec, positions_next: Tensor<B,2>, grad_summands: Tensor<B,2>, accept_mask: Tensor<B,1,Bool>, accept_logp: Tensor<B,1>, h_current: Tensor<B,1>, h_proposed: Tensor<B,1>) -> tuple[StateModelSpec, object]

nodes.name: RunSamplingLoop
nodes.description: Drive warmup/discard and collection iterations by repeatedly applying the HMC transition kernel; produces trace and collected samples.
nodes.type_signature: (hmc_state_in: StateModelSpec, n_collect: int, n_discard: int) -> tuple[array[float], object, StateModelSpec]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Two-Phase Markov State Evolution and Sample Extraction Loop. Iteratively propagates an immutable state through a stochastic transition operator for a fixed number of steps, splitting execution into a non-retained adaptation phase and a retained measurement phase. At each step, the full state is updated and threaded forward, while selected scalar/vector components are conditionally recorded only during the collection window. The procedure returns both the retained sequence and an aggregate diagnostic record spanning all iterations.. Applications: Molecular configuration exploration: discard transient equilibration steps, then retain configurations for ensemble property estimation., Power-grid uncertainty analysis: run state-transition simulations, ignore initialization transients, and retain steady-regime trajectories for risk metrics., Autonomous navigation belief propagation: evolve probabilistic state beliefs, drop early convergence steps, and collect posterior samples for planning., Climate system parameter inference: execute stochastic parameter-state updates, discard burn-in, and retain draws for credible interval construction.

nodes.name: DeriveSamplingShape
nodes.description: Read chain count and latent dimension from the incoming HMC state to define tensor geometry for collection.

inputs.name: hmc_state_in
inputs.constraints: contains current positions with shape [n_chains, dim]

nodes.type_signature: (hmc_state_in: StateModelSpec) -> tuple[int, int]

nodes.name: AllocateCollectionBuffer
nodes.description: Create an empty collection tensor to hold all post-burn-in states before final layout conversion.

outputs.name: buffer_raw
outputs.constraints: uninitialized/empty values to be filled stepwise

nodes.type_signature: (n_collect: int, n_chains: int, dim: int) -> Tensor[float; n_collect, n_chains, dim]

nodes.name: ApplyBurnInTransitions
nodes.description: Advance the HMC kernel for discard iterations and return the post-burn-in state.
nodes.type_signature: (hmc_state_in: StateModelSpec, n_discard: int) -> StateModelSpec

nodes.name: CollectPostBurnInTrajectory
nodes.description: Run n_collect HMC transitions, write each state into the collection buffer, and accumulate per-step trace metrics.

outputs.name: buffer_filled
outputs.constraints: row i contains positions after i-th collection transition

outputs.name: trace_raw
outputs.constraints: per-step diagnostics (e.g., accept-prob/rhat updates, warning events)

outputs.name: hmc_state_out
outputs.constraints: final state after burn-in + collection transitions

nodes.type_signature: (hmc_state_burned_in: StateModelSpec, buffer_raw: Tensor[float; n_collect, n_chains, dim], n_collect: int, n_chains: int, dim: int) -> tuple[Tensor[float; n_collect, n_chains, dim], object, StateModelSpec]

nodes.name: FinalizeOutputs
nodes.description: Convert collected tensor to chain-major layout and derive final run trace/statistics object.

inputs.name: trace_raw
inputs.constraints: may include partial diagnostics and errors

nodes.type_signature: (buffer_filled: Tensor[float; n_collect, n_chains, dim], trace_raw: object) -> tuple[Tensor[float; n_chains, n_collect, dim], object]
